description: Approved Quality Gates that a First Viewing Loop release candidate must satisfy before its GitHub Release. This file is the machine-readable source of truth consumed by scripts/quality_gate_report.py and by the release workflow.

waiver_policy.summary: Critical accessibility, security, privacy, data-integrity, crash-free-playback, and First Viewing Loop failures always block release and can never be waived. Only a lower-severity performance or device-specific failure may ship under a time-limited waiver.

gates.title: First Viewing Loop app-level acceptance suite
conditions: Completes server setup, discovery through Home/library/search, details, playback, track selection, exit, restart, and server-synced resume.
Runs green on the required window matrix (compact API 26 phone lane and expanded API 36 tablet lane).

gates.title: Adaptive Layout journey-state preservation
conditions: Compact portrait/landscape, medium split-screen, expanded tablet portrait/landscape, and folded/unfolded/hinge-occluded foldable configurations preserve journey state through live transitions.
Window-characteristic policy (not device-name detection) keeps content clear of separating folds.

gates.title: Accessibility conformance (WCAG AA)
conditions: Automated accessibility checks pass (Espresso AccessibilityChecks + ComposeAccessibilityValidator).
48dp minimum touch targets with 8dp separation; meaning never conveyed by color alone; reduced-motion honored.
Manual phone and expanded TalkBack passes recorded at largest standard font/display scaling.

gates.title: Playback endurance and progress integrity
conditions: 30-cycle playback endurance suite has zero crashes, ANRs, stuck sessions, or lost positions.
Resume lands within 10 seconds of the last server-acknowledged progress.

evidence.ref: .github/workflows/release.yml#endurance (30 iterations of the recreation + lifecycle suite)

gates.title: API 26 representative-hardware performance thresholds
conditions: Automated: cold-start launch time on API 26 stays within the documented threshold (client time only; the deterministic harness makes no server round-trip).

Runbook-measured on representative API 26 hardware: feedback, cached-navigation, frame-deadline, memory-growth, and artwork/playback responsiveness satisfy the documented thresholds, with server time reported separately. A miss is waivable per the release policy.

evidence.ref: quality-gate/performance.json (produced by the release performance step)

gates.title: Supported Server Line contract compatibility
conditions: Contract suites pass for the latest stable and previous minor Supported Server Lines.
Cover transport, authentication, browsing, playback, tracks, progress, and version-tolerance cases.

gates.title: Privacy — artifacts exclude sensitive data
conditions: Logs, crash reports, diagnostics, and release artifacts exclude secrets, tokens, full media URLs, subtitle contents, and personal library metadata.

gates.title: Install and upgrade smoke
conditions: The signed release APK installs cleanly on API 26 representative configuration.
It upgrades in place over a prior install of itself and launches to a stable activity without crash or ANR.

gates.title: Dependency and secret scans
conditions: Dependency vulnerability scan (Trivy, HIGH/CRITICAL, no ignore-unfixed) reports no findings.
Secret scan (gitleaks) over the checkout reports no leaked secrets.

gates.title: Release integrity and provenance
conditions: A tagged clean CI run produces a signed release APK, SHA-256 checksums, build provenance attestation, SBOM, and a dependency/license report.
This machine-readable Quality Gate report is generated and no critical failure is waived.
